metadata.name: pytranscripts
metadata.summary: A python package for extracting electronic health transcripts ,  and then classifying them based on human annotated data.

functions.name: pytranscripts.utils.get_interviewee_responses
documentation: Returns a list of interviewee responses, this function is only used for cleaning annotated datasets by the human annotators

methods.name: pytranscripts.training.TranscriptTrainer.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the TranscriptTrainer

Args:
    input_file (str): Path to the input Excel file
    destination_path (str, optional): Path to save model outputs and results
    max_length (int, optional): Maximum sequence length for tokenization
    num_train_epochs (int, optional): Number of training epochs
    learning_rate_distilbert (float, optional): Learning rate for DistilBERT
    learning_rate_electra (float, optional): Learning rate for Electra
    labels (List[str], optional): List of classification labels
    top_levels (Dict[str, List[str]], optional): Dictionary mapping top-level categories to their subcategories

methods.name: pytranscripts.training.TranscriptTrainer._load_and_prepare_data
documentation: Load input data and prepare for training

methods.name: pytranscripts.training.TranscriptTrainer._classify_text
documentation:
Classify a single text using a pipeline model

Args:
    text (str): Text to classify
    pipeline_model (pipeline): Trained pipeline model

Returns:
    dict: Classification result with additional top-level information

methods.name: pytranscripts.training.TranscriptTrainer.classify_using_model
documentation:
Classify text using a trained model

Args:
    model_path (str): Path to the trained model
    input_dataframe (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame to classify

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: Classified DataFrame

methods.name: pytranscripts.training.TranscriptTrainer.train_and_classify
documentation:
Train both models and run classification

Returns:
    tuple: Trained DistilBERT and Electra models

methods.name: pytranscripts.training.TranscriptTrainer._get_latest_checkpoint
documentation:
Get the latest checkpoint from a model directory

Args:
    model_dir (str): Directory containing model checkpoints

Returns:
    str: Path to the latest checkpoint

functions.name: pytranscripts.core.docx_transcripts_to_excel
documentation:
This function reads all .docx files from the specified directory,
processes interview data, and exports it to an Excel file.

Parameters:
input_directory (str): Directory containing the de-identified transcripts in .docx format.
output_file (str): The path where the resulting Excel file should be saved.

Returns:
None: Saves the processed data into an Excel file.
